free_text: FROM NEUTRON INDUCED REACTIONS IN CSI-
LOADED TITANIUM FOIL OF 10MICROM THICKNESS IN
A THIN WALLED (1MM) STEEL REACTION CHAMBER,
NEUTRON FLUX WAS 5*10+7 PER SECOND IN 4PI.
.SAME AS DETECTOR, CANNED IN TANTALUM AND AL
LINED WITH A TI-O2 REFLECTING LAYER.
BY 1IN. HEIGHT ON 56AVP TUBES 25-50CM FROM THE TI-T
TARGET AT 15DEG AND 50DEG ANGLES FOR NEUTRON
ANGLE WITH A 1IN. DIAM. BY 1.5IN. STILBENE CRYSTAL
SPECTROMETER ON A 56AVP TUBE - FOR FLUX.
NORMALISATION WAS DONE BY INTEGRATION OF
FROM DIFFERENT REACTION MECHANISMS WERE CARRIED OUT
USING THE OPTICAL, STATISTICAL AND A HYBRID
UNCERTAINTY IN ANGULAR POSITION OF THE CSI CRYSTALS
.THE ENERGY RESOLUTION OF THE DETECTOR WAS  12PC FOR
SYSTEMATIC ERROR. FROM FINITE SIZE OF THE
CS-I CRYTALS AND THE ANGULAR ERROR   6-9 PERCENT.
